# Getting Support for Open Source Contribution Atelier

Welcome to the **Open Source Contribution Atelier**! Whether you are a student learning Git, an SSOC 2026 contributor working on issues, or a maintainer managing the project, we are here to help you.

Please use the appropriate channels below depending on your needs.

---

## 💬 Ask a Question (Non-Bug Support)
If you have a general question about how to use the platform, need help understanding a Git lesson, or want to discuss a new feature idea:
- **GitHub Discussions**: Use the [GitHub Discussions Board](https://github.com/goyaljiiiiii/Open-Source-Contribution-Atelier/discussions) (if enabled) to ask community questions and get guidance from mentors and peers.
- **SSOC Discord / Slack**: If you are part of the SSOC 2026 cohort, join the official community workspace channels (e.g., `#atelier-atelier`) to chat with assigned mentors (PAs) in real-time.

---

## 🐛 Report a Bug or Request a Feature
If you encountered a reproducible error on the platform or want to request a new curriculum module:
- **First, Search Existing Issues**: Check the [Issues Search](https://github.com/goyaljiiiiii/Open-Source-Contribution-Atelier/issues) to see if someone else has already reported it.
- **File a Bug Report**: Open a new issue using our **Bug Report Template**. Provide system details, browser versions, and terminal reproduction logs.
- **Request a Feature**: Open a new issue using our **Feature Request Template** outlining the educational benefits and user story of the proposed enhancement.

---

## 📖 Self-Help & Documentation
Before asking for help, please review the existing resources:
1. **[README.md](README.md)**: Main configuration guide, manual development setup instructions, and quick start scripts.
2. **[CONTENT_GUIDE.md](CONTENT_GUIDE.md)**: Guide on how to add curriculum modules, parse markdown files, and format interactive quizzes.
3. **[CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)**: Details on the contributor lifecycle, branch naming, local testing, and PR verification.
4. **[SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md)**: Vulnerability disclosure guidelines and reporting channels.

---

## 🧑‍🤝‍🧑 Contribution Community Etiquette
Please remember that this is a community project managed by volunteer maintainers. Always:
- Be respectful and follow our [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).
- Do not ping maintainers directly on personal social media or private messages for support on assigned issues.
- Give maintainers and mentors at least **24–48 hours** to review questions, issue comments, or PR submissions.
